title: Business Development Assistant
description:
Company: Chasm Opportunities LLC

Founder: David Madden, CEO & Founder

Location: Remote (Philippines-based candidates preferred)

Hours: Part-time, ~4 hours per week to start; long-term with growth potential

Compensation: Negotiable

Chasm Opportunities (CO) is a technology-transition consulting firm supporting ~14 early-stage startup clients in government, defense, energy, and commercial markets. The Principal works directly with each client on an ongoing, fractional-support basis, helping them identify and pursue business development opportunities. We are hiring a part-time, remote BD Assistant to support lead generation, outreach drafting, funding opportunity research, and marketing material improvement across our client portfolio.

About the work

Our clients are very early-stage technology startups commercializing products into government and commercial markets — defense, energy, advanced manufacturing, logistics, and adjacent sectors. They each have a real product or capability but limited BD infrastructure. Your job is to help CO find the right people and organizations for each client, draft the first outreach, monitor relevant funding opportunities, and identify where their marketing materials can be sharpened.

You will work directly with CO's Principal and Executive Assistant, track all activity in Monday CRM, and operate as a disciplined, organized extension of a lean team.

What you'll do

1. Lead research and identification

You will receive general search direction from the Principal (e.g., "Identify relevant POCs within Air Force Training Command who oversee simulation and training systems acquisition") and independently research and build a qualified lead list. This includes: 

• Searching government directories, LinkedIn, agency websites, acquisition portals (e.g., 
, military small business sites), and relevant industry sources.

• Identifying specific individuals: name, title, organization, program office or division,
/phone where available, and a brief rationale for why they are a relevant target.

• Flagging warm-connection opportunities (e.g., mutual contacts, prior engagement) where they exist.

• Entering all leads cleanly into Monday CRM following the established board structure and field conventions.

2. Outreach drafting, tracking, and follow-up management

You will draft outreach
s for the Principal's review and approval before anything is sent, and then maintain clean, current records of all outreach activity in Monday CRM. This includes: 

• Drafting concise, professional, tailored outreach
s — warm introductions, cold outreach to targeted prospects, and follow-up
s.

• Clearly communicating each client's value proposition in plain language, with no jargon or generic pitches.

• Logging each touchpoint 
 sent, response received, follow-up needed) against the correct contact and client account in Monday CRM.

• Providing a short biweekly status summary: what went out, what came back, and what needs attention.

3. Funding opportunity research via RSS feed monitoring

You will build and maintain a structured RSS feed monitoring system to surface relevant grant and funding opportunities for each client. This includes: 

• Developing a tailored keyword set for each client based on their technology domain, market focus, and target funding sources (e.g., SBIR/STTR, DOE, DOD, ARPA-E, commercial grants).

• Monitoring active RSS feeds from relevant agencies, databases, and publications on a regular cadence and flagging any opportunities that match a client's keywords and profile.

• Presenting matched opportunities to the Principal with a brief summary: funding source, opportunity title, relevance to the client, deadline, and a link to the full posting.

• Managing the feed library actively: adding new feeds as new clients are onboarded or new funding sources become relevant, and removing feeds that are no longer applicable as the client portfolio evolves.

• Keeping the feed and keyword library documented and current so it remains an asset that reflects the current portfolio at all times.

4. Marketing material review and improvement suggestions

Each client has some combination of a website, flyers, and/or quad charts — most are early-stage and need polish. You will: 

• Review existing materials for clarity, consistency, and effectiveness in communicating the client's capability and market fit.

• Identify specific, actionable improvements: unclear messaging, missing calls to action, misaligned audience framing, design inconsistencies, or content gaps.

• Provide written feedback in plain language that the client or CO can act on.

• You are not expected to produce final design deliverables — your role is to provide clear, thoughtful critique and recommendations.

What we're looking for

• Experience in B2B or government-facing business development, lead research, or sales support — particularly in defense or government contracting contexts.

• Strong research skills: ability to find the right person in a large government or corporate organization with minimal starting information, and to identify relevant funding opportunities from public sources.

• Excellent written English: you will be drafting outreach on behalf of a senior consultant to government and industry contacts, so professional tone and precision matter greatly.

• Demonstrated experience building and managing RSS feeds or monitoring systems for opportunity tracking, grant research, or competitive intelligence.

• Comfort working in Monday CRM (or a similar CRM); the architecture is already designed — you must be organized and disciplined about keeping records clean and current.

• Ability to give constructive, specific feedback on marketing materials.

• Self-directed and reliable in an async, remote environment.

Engagement details

• Type: Part-time, freelance

• Work style: Fully remote, primarily async with periodic check-ins with the Principal and Executive Assistant.

• Starting scope: ~4 hours per week, with expectation to grow as the role proves out

• Timeline: Long-term engagement for the right candidate

How to apply

Please provide:

1. A brief description of a lead research project you've completed — what the target audience was, how you found leads, and what tools you used.

2. A short sample or description of an outreach
 you drafted for an industry contact on behalf of a technology company, and how you tracked and managed follow-up.

3. Your experience monitoring RSS feeds, grant databases, or opportunity pipelines — what tools you used, how you organized the results, and how you communicated matches to your team or client.

4. Any additional information you want to share to show how your capabilities align with this job posting.

5. Your currently hourly rate and availability.

title: Google Ads media buyer — lead gen

Why Join Us

Full autonomy: You run the accounts. We trust your judgment.

Growth path: Start part-time, scale to full-time, move into a lead/management role as we grow.



What You'll Do

Build and own Search, Performance Max, and Display strategies tuned for CPL and lead quality.

Develop keyword strategies, ad copy frameworks, and landing page hypotheses that drive qualified form fills and calls.

Optimize toward CPL, lead-to-close rate, and blended CAC — not vanity metrics like CTR or impressions.

Structure campaigns for scale (TOFU prospecting, BOFU retargeting, branded vs non-branded separation).

Identify and execute scaling opportunities — don't just maintain spend.

Bonus Points

eting

title: Electrical Estimator & Process Lead
description:
About Us:

We are a fast-growing Australian solar and electrical company launching a dedicated electrical division. We need a proactive and experienced VA Electrical Estimator who can take full ownership of establishing and running our electrical quoting and job coordination processes under strategic oversight.

• Identify process improvements and implement best practices proactively

• Provide regular updates and reports to management on quoting accuracy, job status, and profitability

Requirements:

• Proven experience managing electrical quoting and estimation, preferably in a remote or virtual role

• Demonstrated ability to design and lead operational processes with minimal supervision

• Excellent English communication and strong organizational skills

• High initiative, accountability, and problem-solving mindset

• Familiarity with Simpro or similar software highly preferred

• Comfortable working independently and collaborating remotely

Hours: Flexible with overlap in Australian business hours; full-time preferred

Benefits:

• KPI-driven incentives rewarding quoting accuracy, job profitability, and operational excellence

• Flexible remote work arrangement

• Chance to shape and lead a fast-growing electrical division

• Potential for long-term leadership career advancement

• Stable full-time position - 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday, with weekends off

• Opportunity to join a reputable Australian company with strong prospects for professional growth

title: SOCIAL MEDIA VA – Instagram Reels Editor & Automation Specialist
description:
SOCIAL MEDIA VA – Instagram Reels Editor & Automation Specialist

Part-Time | Long-Term | Remote

---

ABOUT THE ACCOUNT

We run an Instagram account (22K followers) supporting couples through the season of early parenthood — the relationship strain, the identity shifts, the beautiful hard work of staying connected while raising babies. The content is warm, psychologically grounded, and genuinely helpful. Our audience is engaged and growing.



The face of the account is transitioning to a licensed psychologist and couples therapist. You'll be helping bring her voice and expertise to life on Instagram.

---

WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

Video Editing (this is the core of the role)

• Receive raw talking-head footage filmed on an iPhone

• Edit into polished Instagram Reels (cuts, captions, music, pacing)

• Our style is clean, warm, and professional — not trendy or overly produced

• Deliver 2-4 edited Reels per week



Posting & Scheduling

• Post content to Instagram on a set schedule

• Write or finalize captions using provided scripts and brand voice

• Add relevant hashtags



Carousel Design

• Design 1–2 carousels per week in Canva using our existing templates

• Content and copy will be provided — you bring the visual execution



Automation Management

• Maintain and monitor our ManyChat comment-trigger flows

• Manage our Flodesk or Kit
 sequences — tagging, list hygiene, basic troubleshooting

• Flag anything that needs attention



Community

• Monitor comments and DMs

• Respond to straightforward comments using provided guidelines

• Flag comments that need a personal response from the psychologist



---

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

• Proven Instagram Reels editing experience — please include a portfolio or examples

• Comfortable with Canva, ManyChat, and Kit/Flodesk (or very willing to learn)

• Excellent written English — your caption writing will represent a licensed professional

• Reliable, proactive, and detail-oriented

• Someone who genuinely cares about the mission — this content helps real couples who are new parents

• Available for a short weekly check-in via voice or video



---

WHAT THIS IS NOT

This is not a high-volume, post-10-times-a-week account. This is a focused, quality-first account where every piece of content matters. We're looking for someone who takes pride in their work and wants to grow with us long-term.



---



HOURS & PAY

• Approximately 8–10 hours per week to start

• Long-term position with room to grow as the account and business expand

• Please include your hourly rate when you apply

---

HOW TO APPLY

Please send:

1. A short introduction (3–5 sentences) about your experience with Instagram content

2. 2–3 examples of Reels you have edited

3. Your familiarity with ManyChat and Kit (honest is fine — we can train the right person)

4. Your availability and hourly rate



Applications without Reels examples will not be reviewed. We will respond to every serious application within 3 business days. 



We look forward to talking with you! 

-Sarah & Tucker

Work from home

title: Insurance Claims Operations Specialist (Contract) — Product Testing
description:
We're building AI tools for insurance claims teams. We need someone who's lived this work to help us test and refine the product.



You're a fit if you've:



Worked in a BPO or shared services center handling insurance claims operations for a US or European insurer, MGA, or TPA. You've personally touched first notice of loss (FNOL) intake, claims adjudication, policy servicing, endorsements, audits, renewals, or submission processing. You know what breaks in the real workflow: the missing documentation, the edge-case escalations, the manual data entry across back-office systems, and the quality gaps that slip through.



What you'd do:



Test our product against real-world insurance claims scenarios and integrations. Flag edge features we're missing. Pressure test the system — especially around document processing, data extraction, and business logic rules. Help with product design. Build demos with real use cases and data.



Requirements:



- 3+ years hands-on insurance claims or policy operations in a BPO or shared services setting for a US or European insurance carrier, MGA, or TPA.

- Direct experience with claims management systems, document intake workflows, and back-office processing tools.

- Familiarity with FNOL handling, claims file preparation, or policy servicing workflows.

- Strong English communication.

- Comfortable giving blunt, specific feedback.



Bonus if you have experience with:

- AI-assisted document processing or automation tools in insurance.

- Quality assurance or human-in-the-loop verification processes.

- Working with APIs or system integrations in an insurance context.



How to apply:



Feel free to message me directly on WhatsApp at 
for a fast reply. Please include your resume or LinkedIn, your OnlineJobs.ph profile link, and a short note about your past insurance claims experience and why you think you'd be a good fit for this role.

title: Sales & Operations VA
description:
About Gutter Guru

Gutter Guru is a growing commercial gutter cleaning company serving property managers, HOAs, and commercial clients across the country. We connect customers with a trusted network of local subcontractors and pride ourselves on reliable service, clear communication, and professional results. As we scale, we're looking for a detail-oriented tea
mber to support our sales and operations pipeline.



Role Overview

The Sales & Operations Coordinator plays a critical behind-the-scenes role in keeping Gutter Guru's growth engine running. You'll own our outbound lead generation, daily
 outreach, contact data management, and financial tracking — ensuring our pipeline stays full and our books stay clean. This is a high-accountability role for someone who is organized, persistent, and comfortable working across multiple tools and workflows simultaneously.



Key Responsibilities



Lead Generation

- Research and identify potential leads using LinkedIn and Apollo based on target customer criteria

- Collect key contact information including name, company, and
 address

- Add and maintain leads in the designated Google Sheets folder, ensuring all records are accurate, complete, and properly formatted for outreach campaigns

- Keep lead lists clean and organized for use in YAMM mail merge campaigns



Daily
 Outreach (YAMM)

- Execute daily outbound
 campaigns using YAMM (Yet Another Mail Merge) via Gmail — approximately 50–60
s per sending account per day

- Set up correct
 templates and verify merge fields are accurate before each send

- Review YAMM reports after each campaign and record key metrics 
s sent, opens, replies, etc.) in the tracking sheet

- Maintain organized documentation of all outreach results for reporting and performance review



Phone Number Research

- Work through existing contact lists to locate and add missing phone numbers

- Source numbers by reviewing
 conversations, signatures, and other available records

- Update Pipedrive CRM with all discovered contact information



Subcontractor Payments

- Process payments to subcontractors and track payment schedules

- Confirm subcontractors are paid accurately and on time

- Maintain organized payment records for internal reporting



Accounts Receivable Management

- Monitor outstanding customer invoices and follow up on pending or overdue balances

- Communicate professionally with customers regarding payment status

- Record received payments in both the CRM and QuickBooks to keep financial records up to date



What We're Looking For

- Highly organized with strong attention to detail — data accuracy is non-negotiable

- Comfortable working with tools like Google Sheets, LinkedIn, Apollo, YAMM, Pipedrive, and QuickBooks

- Self-starter who can manage daily workflows independently without heavy oversight

- Clear communicator, both written and verbal

- Prior experience in lead generation, sales support, or administrative operations is a plus



Why Gutter Guru

- Flexible remote work environment

- Clear processes and workflows — you'll know exactly what success looks like

- A growing company where your contributions have a direct impact

- Collaborative, no-nonsense team culture



Please apply using the link below:

title: Customer Success & Sales Support Manager
description:
Remote | Full-Time | Must work US Pacific Time hours

Compensation: $1,500–$2,500 USD/month to start, depending on fit and experience, plus performance bonuses and growth upside



About Us



We’re a fast-growing founder-led wellness brand based in Las Vegas. We’ve built strong traction, loyal customers, and a product people genuinely love.



Now we’re hiring a sharp, highly organized, customer-facing operator to help us drive more revenue, improve retention, and create more opportunities from behind the scenes.



This is not a passive admin role. You will play a direct part in helping the business grow.



The Role



You’ll sit at the intersection of customer success, sales support, outreach, and CRM ownership.



Our local team is out in the market selling, building relationships, and creating 
ntum. Your job is to back that up online by making sure leads are followed up with, customers are taken care of, opportunities keep moving, and nothing slips through the cracks.



You will help create growth through:

 • lead follow-up

 • customer support

 • onboarding

 • partner and affiliate outreach

 • CRM management

 • follow-through and pipeline support



You are not just here to “assist.”

You are here to help turn attention into revenue, customers into long-term fans, and conversations into real opportunities.



How Performance Is Measured



You will be judged on 3 things:



1. More Revenue

How well you help move leads, follow-ups, and opportunities toward real sales.



2. Better Retention & Customer Experience

How well you create a smooth customer journey, solve issues quickly, and keep customers engaged and happy.



3. More Opportunities & Cleaner Pipeline

How well you help generate opportunities through outreach and follow-through while keeping the CRM organized and actionable.



Core KPIs may include:

 • follow-up speed and consistency

 • opportunities created

 • meetings booked

 • revenue influenced

 • onboarding quality

 • retention / repeat orders

 • CRM accuracy and pipeline hygiene



What You’ll Do

 • Follow up with warm leads, prospects, and customers

 • Manage customer communication across text,
, phone, and CRM

 • Support onboarding and customer success

 • Reach out to affiliates, partners, and collaboration targets online

 • Keep the CRM, notes, and follow-up tasks updated

 • Support post-meeting follow-up and coordination

 • Help improve templates, workflows, automations, and internal organization

 • Make sure opportunities keep moving and nothing gets dropped



Who You Are

 • Strong spoken and written English

 • Warm, polished, and professional in communication

 • Highly organized and detail-oriented

 • Strong with follow-up and naturally proactive

 • Comfortable with customer service, sales support, and outreach

 • Confident using CRM systems like GoHighLevel, Close, HubSpot, or similar

 • Comfortable using AI tools to move faster and work smarter

 • Able to manage multiple moving parts without losing track

 • Available full-time during US Pacific Time business hours



Strong Plus If You Have

 • Experience in customer success, executive support, account management, sales support, or business development

 • Experience with lead follow-up and pipeline management

 • Experience with affiliate outreach, online partnerships, or social media outreach

 • Experience in wellness, hospitality, consumer brands, or relationship-driven businesses

 • Experience building workflows, SOPs, automations, or better systems



Important — Read Before Applying



Please do not apply unless you can follow instructions exactly.



This role requires strong communication, attention to detail, follow-through, and customer-facing professionalism. The application process is part of the evaluation.



If you do not follow the instructions below exactly, your application will be dismissed without review.



This includes:

 • missing the Loom video

 • going over the time limit

 • skipping any required question

 • ignoring the exact submission format

 • unclear spoken English for a customer-facing role

 • lack of availability during US Pacific Time business hours



This is a customer-facing role. You must have clear, professional spoken English and be comfortable on calls, voice notes, and video.



How to Apply



Send one complete application with these 3 items:



1. A Loom video (maximum 2 minutes)



Start by saying:



“My name is [Your Name], I’m in [Your City], and today is [Date] at [Time].”



Then answer these 2 questions:

 • Tell me about a time you helped create revenue or opportunity through follow-up, outreach, customer communication, or account management. What did you do, and what was the result?

 • This role involves customer success, lead follow-up, outreach, CRM ownership, and sales support. How would you structure your first 30 days to create 
ntum and make sure nothing slips through the cracks?



2. A short paragraph

Tell us why you are a strong fit for this role.



3. One sentence only

Complete this sentence exactly:



“The KPI I’d be most excited to own in this role is ______.”



Only complete applications will be reviewed.



What Happens Next



If your application shows strong communication, ownership, organization, and follow-through, the next step will be a Zoom interview and a short paid test project.



We move fast and are looking for someone who can do the same.

title: Senior Lending Intelligence & Bank Relationship Specialist (Philippines Only – Remote)
description:
READ FIRST – Not a Basic VA Role



We are hiring someone in the Philippines with real lending experience and real banking connections.



This role is for someone who has:

 • Worked inside a bank, credit union, lending company, or broker office (USA)

 • Built relationships with branch managers, relationship managers, or business banking reps

 • Seen high-limit approvals happen firsthand

 • Wants long-term stability, growth, and performance-based income



If you have experience working with banks like Chase, Fifth Third, or similar U.S. institutions — especially handling business credit cards or business lines of credit — this opportunity is for you.



?



What We Are Looking For



We want someone who:

 • Has access to or relationships with branch managers or business banking representatives

 • Understands how approvals are influenced for:

 • $50K+ business credit cards

 • $75K–$100K business lines of credit

 • High-limit unsecured funding

 • Has firsthand knowledge of approval behavior (not theory)



Examples of the types of approvals we are targeting:

 • $50,000+ business credit cards

 • $100,000 business lines of credit

 • High-limit unsecured credit through relationship banking



If you have worked around these approvals, we want to speak with you.

Compensation Structure



This is not just a fixed salary role.



You will receive:

 • Competitive base pay

 • Commissions when clients get approved

 • Large bonuses when clients secure high funding amounts

 • Long-term growth opportunity



This position is designed for someone who wants a professional home — a place to grow, be valued, and build something long-term.



If you perform well and help drive approvals, your income grows with it.



Responsibilities

 • Document real lender approval patterns based on your experience

 • Help identify which banks are currently approving higher limits

 • Leverage relationships with bank reps or branch managers when appropriate

 • Track approval tendencies by:

 • Credit score

 • Income

 • Business revenue

 • Bank deposits

 • Assist in structuring borrower profiles to increase approval odds

 • Build and maintain an internal lender intelligence database



Required Background (Must Have At Least One)

 • Former Bank Loan Processor

 • Former Loan Underwriter

 • Business Banking Assistant

 • Relationship Manager Support Staff

 • Lending Operations Staff

 • Broker Submission Manager



We are not hiring entry-level virtual assistants for this role.



Major Advantage (Priority Consideration)

You will receive priority if you:

 • Have direct contact with business banking reps

 • Have access to branch managers

 • Previously assisted with high-limit approvals

 • Worked inside U.S.-based banks or credit unions

 • Can explain how business lines of credit are actually approved



Screening Questions (Required – Be Specific)

 1. Have you worked directly with branch managers or relationship managers? Explain your role.

 2. What is the highest business credit card limit you personally saw approved?

 3. What borrower profile typically qualifies for $50K+ business credit cards?

 4. What does a borrower need to qualify for a $75K–$100K line of credit?

 5. Do you currently maintain any banking contacts?



Generic answers will not be considered.

How to Apply



Submit:

 • Your resume

 • Answers to all screening questions

 • Your WhatsApp number

 • Your
 address



Applications missing any of the above will not be reviewed.

Final Note



We are building a serious funding operation. This role is for experienced professionals who understand how approvals truly work and want to grow with a long-term team.



If you have real insider knowledge and real banking relationships, this opportunity can be highly rewarding.

title: Chief Marketing Officer
description:
About Us



We are a US-based event ticket marketplace on a mission to create a more efficient and seamless selling experience.



As we continue to grow, we’re looking for a Chief Marketing Officer who can take full ownership of our marketing function, bring structure to the team, and drive measurable growth across the business.



This is a great opportunity for a senior marketing leader who enjoys building strategy, leading teams, improving performance, and making a real impact in a fast-moving company.



Why This Role Matters



Right now, we need someone who can step in and become the clear owner of marketing.



We’re looking for a leader who can set the direction, align the team, improve accountability, and ensure our marketing efforts are tied to real business outcomes. You’ll play a key role in helping the company scale by turning marketing into a more focused, data-driven, and performance-oriented function.



What You’ll Be Doing



As our Chief Marketing Officer, you will lead marketing end to end, from high-level strategy to day-to-day direction.



Your responsibilities will include:



Marketing Strategy & Leadership

• Own and lead the overall marketing strategy for the business

• Turn company goals into a clear and actionable marketing roadmap

• Identify the highest-impact growth opportunities across channels

• Set priorities, timelines, and budgets for marketing initiatives

• Ensure the marketing team is aligned, productive, and focused on what matters most



Team Management & Direction

• Lead and manage the existing marketing team

• Provide clear direction, structure, and accountability

• Help the team move beyond just execution by creating better focus and strategic clarity

• Identify gaps in the team and recommend or hire additional support such as freelancers, agencies, or channel specialists when needed



Growth & Demand Generation

• Oversee multi-channel marketing efforts including:

• SEO

• Paid acquisition

• Outbound growth

• Content

• Other performance-driven channels

• Improve lead generation, customer acquisition, and conversion performance

• Ensure all channels work together within one cohesive growth strategy

• Review current funnels, campaigns, and processes to improve underperforming areas



Performance, Metrics & Accountability

• Define KPIs, dashboards, and reporting systems for the marketing function

• Track ROI and ensure marketing spend is tied to measurable outcomes

• Run regular performance reviews and team check-ins

• Build a culture of testing, learning, and data-backed decision-making

• Improve visibility, organization, and accountability across the department



Cross-Functional Collaboration

• Work closely with leadership to align marketing with business goals

• Collaborate with sales and other teams to strengthen pipeline generation and messaging

• Help refine positioning, go-to-market priorities, and channel strategy based on market feedback and performance data



Who We’re Looking For



You may be a strong fit for this role if you are:

• A strategic marketing leader who also knows how to execute

• Comfortable taking ownership with minimal supervision

• Strong at leading teams, setting priorities, and building structure

• Deeply performance-driven and confident using data to guide decisions

• Experienced in managing multiple channels and tying marketing activity back to growth



Requirements



• 7+ years of experience in marketing

• Significant experience in a senior or leadership-level marketing role

• Proven success leading multi-channel marketing strategies and teams

• Strong experience in performance marketing, growth, paid ads, SEO, and demand generation

• Experience building structure, accountability, and reporting into marketing operations

• Strong analytical mindset and ability to measure what’s working

• Excellent communication and leadership skills

• Comfortable working in a fast-paced, evolving environment

• Able to operate independently and drive progress without constant oversight



Nice to Have



• Experience in marketplaces, e-commerce, ticketing, consumer internet, or transactional platforms

• Experience managing internal teams alongside agencies, freelancers, or specialists

• Background in scaling marketing functions for high-growth companies

• Familiarity with outbound or lifecycle-driven growth strategies



What Success Looks Like



In this role, success means:

• Marketing has a clear owner and strategic direction

• The team is focused, well-managed, and accountable

• SEO, paid, outbound, and content efforts are aligned and performance-driven

• Marketing spend is carefully tracked and tied to results

• Leadership has visibility into KPIs, progress, and next steps

• Marketing moves forward effectively without needing constant founder oversight



Why Join Us



• Work with a growing US-based company

• Take ownership of a critical leadership function

title: Part-Time Digital Advertising Specialist
description:

You will focus on platform-specific strategies to capture high-quality inquiries online under strategic guidance from senior marketing leadership.

• Test and manage leads generation activities on Australian lead marketplaces such as ServiceSeeking and Hipages

• Support and coordinate with Google My Business and local SEO efforts to amplify ad performance

• Collaborate with the marketing lead and VA estimator to ensure smooth lead flow and campaign alignment

• Provide detailed weekly reports with performance insights and optimization recommendations

Requirements:

• Demonstrable experience managing successful paid ad campaigns on Meta, Google, and LinkedIn

• Knowledge of lead generation strategies across residential and commercial sectors

• Ability to work independently in a remote environment and meet deadlines

• Experience with Australian online lead marketplaces is a plus

title: Meta & Google Ads Media Buyer — DTC Brands (Remote | Long-Term)
description:
SMCA Creative Agency is a US-based performance marketing agency managing paid media for DTC and ecommerce brands. We're looking for a sharp, self-directed media buyer who can own Meta campaigns from strategy to execution — and make our existing strategies better, not just follow them.

Account spend ranges from $2,500 to $30,000 per month. You need to know how to work both ends of that range.

This is a long-term contractor role. We don't hire people who need to be managed.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN



Full Meta Ads management — strategy, build, launch, optimize

Google Ads support — primarily Search and Performance Max

Improving on strategies handed to you — we want your thinking, not just your execution

Identifying creative gaps and developing concepts for new ad creative

Weekly performance reporting with clear explanations — not just numbers, but what they mean and what we're doing about it

Communicating proactively when something needs attention





REQUIRED SKILLS



3+ years running Meta Ads for DTC or ecommerce brands

Proven results at account spends between $2,500–$30,000/month

Google Ads experience — Search and/or PMax

Strong understanding of DTC customer acquisition funnels

Creative strategy — you can brief or concept new ad angles, not just manage budgets

Clean, clear reporting — you explain performance like you're talking to a client, not an algorithm

Written English fluency





WHO YOU ARE

You understand that media buying is part science, part instinct. You know when to test, when to scale, and when to kill something. You don't wait for instructions when the data is telling you something. You bring ideas to the table — new angles, new audiences, new creative concepts — because you actually care about the results.

You've worked with DTC brands. You understand offers, hooks, and why creative is the variable that changes everything.



HOW TO APPLY

No generic applications. Answer these four questions:



What DTC brands have you run Meta Ads for? Include spend levels, ROAS or CPA results, and what you did specifically to drive performance

Walk me through how you'd approach a new Meta account at $5,000/month spend for a skincare brand — what are your first 30 days?

How do you identify when creative is the problem versus the targeting or the offer?

What does your weekly reporting process look like? How do you communicate performance to a client who isn't in the ads manager every day?



Applications without answers will not be reviewed.

title: Ad Creative Designer — DTC & Ecommerce Brands (Remote | Long-Term)
description:
SMCA Creative Agency is a US-based performance marketing agency serving DTC and ecommerce brands. We're looking for a versatile creative designer who lives at the intersection of design and performance — someone who understands that every asset they make needs to stop the scroll, communicate the offer, and drive action.You'll be producing static ad creatives across Meta, Google,
, and web. You'll work across multiple client brands simultaneously, each with their own aesthetic and audience.This is a long-term contractor role. If you can't work fast and independently across different brand voices, this isn't the right fit.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN



Static ad creatives for Meta and Google

 design assets

Web design support — banners, landing page sections, image assets

Maintaining brand consistency across multiple client accounts

Writing short-form headlines and hooks — you don't just design around copy, you improve it

Turning a creative brief into a finished asset with minimal back and forth





REQUIRED SKILLS



Figma, Canva, and Adobe Suite — you work in all three

Strong static design fundamentals — hierarchy, typography, color, composition

DTC and ecommerce creative experience — you know what performs, not just what looks good

Basic copy skills — headlines, hooks, short-form ad copy

Ability to manage multiple brand aesthetics simultaneously without blending them

Fast turnaround — landing pages and ad sets can't wait a week

Clear communication in English



BONUS SKILLS



Motion graphics or basic video editing

Understanding of Meta ad formats and spec requirements

 template design experience





WHO YOU ARE

You've made ads that actually worked. You understand the difference between pretty design and performance design. You can take a brief like "DTC skincare brand, female audience 25–45, new hero product launch" and come back with three distinct creative concepts — designed, copy written, ready to test.

You don't need someone to tell you what size to export. You don't need three rounds of revisions to get the concept right. You ask the right questions upfront and deliver clean.



HOW TO APPLY

No generic applications. Answer these three questions:



Share your portfolio — specifically show static ad work for DTC or ecommerce brands across Meta, Google, or

Walk me through how you approach a new creative brief — from receiving direction to delivering final assets

Give an example of a time you improved on a creative concept you were handed — what did you change and why?



Applications without a portfolio and answers will not be reviewed.

title: Content Operations Manager (Full-Time, Remote)
description:
We’re looking for a reliable and detail-oriented Content Operations Manager to organize, systemize, and oversee the day-to-day content flow between our creative, marketing, and editing teams. You’ll make sure all content (videos, images, and assets) is up-to-date, properly labeled, and ready for use across our platforms.



If you love structure, collaboration, and keeping creative teams running smoothly—this role is for you.



Responsibilities

Manage and organize all content inside Google Drive (for “MV” project).

Coordinate between multiple content teams — Outreach Creators, Organic Social Team, Editors, and
 Team.

Ensure each team receives the correct approved assets (images, videos, clips) on time.

Label, track, and store all files systematically to avoid duplication or misplacement.

Maintain and update Asana project management boards — make sure all tasks are assigned, labeled, and followed up.

Double-check that only approved videos go to editors or organic content teams.

Report weekly on content progress, missing assets, and workflow blockers.

Assist with organic content curation and outreach coordination when needed.



Requirements

Strong organizational and communication skills.

Must have experience with Google Drive and Asana.

Comfortable working remotely and managing multiple teams.

Good understanding of digital content production (videos, images, social posts).

High attention to detail — labeling, naming, and sorting accuracy is a must.

Reliable internet connection and clear written English skills.



Nice-to-Have (Bonus Points)

Experience managing content for influencers, creators, or agencies.

Knowledge of basic video formats and file management.

A love for efficient systems and documentation.

Slight “OCD” for perfection in naming files or keeping things orderly — yes, we love that.



Compensation & Schedule

Full-time: 40 hours per week

Rate: $5 USD per hour

Work during Sydney (AEST/AEDT) business hours.



To apply, please include:

Your resume or work experience summary

A short message explaining why you’d be a great fit

A 2-minute Loom video showing how you organize files or manage Asana tasks (optional but preferred)

title: SEO Specialist
description:
We're Not Looking for Someone Who "Does SEO." We're Looking for Someone Who Lives It.



We're building our internal marketing team from the ground up — and the first person through the door needs to be exceptional. We want an SEO specialist who eats algorithm updates for breakfast, who tracks rankings obsessively, and who gets genuine satisfaction from watching organic traffic climb.



If SEO is your craft, your passion, and your competitive edge — read on.



THE ROLE



This is a full-time, long-term position not a short-term contract or a side gig. We're investing in someone who wants to grow with us and make SEO the backbone of our digital presence. You'll have real ownership, real impact, and the freedom to do the work the right way.



You'll be working autonomously so if you need hand-holding, this isn't the role for you. But if you're the type who thrives when given a goal and the freedom to chase it, you'll love it here.



What you'll be doing:

- Owning and executing our full SEO strategy — technical, on-page, and off-page

- Conducting in-depth keyword research and competitive analysis

- Auditing and optimising site structure, page speed, schema, and crawlability

- Building and managing high-quality link acquisition campaigns

- Producing and optimising content that ranks and converts

- Tracking, analysing, and reporting on organic performance with clear, data-driven insights

- Staying ahead of algorithm changes and proactively adapting strategy



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



We want someone who can prove it. Not just talk about SEO — but show us the numbers. Rankings lifted. Traffic grown. Revenue driven. We'll be asking for case studies and real results.



You're the right fit if you:

- Have a proven track record of delivering measurable SEO results (bring the data)

- Live and breathe SEO — you're reading industry news, testing theories, and refining your craft constantly

- Can work independently and manage your own time without micromanagement

- Are deeply skilled in technical SEO — not just content optimisation

- Have strong experience with tools like Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, Google Search Console, and GA4

- Communicate clearly and can translate data into actionable strategy

- Are hungry to build something great, not just maintain the status quo



WHAT YOU WON'T FIND HERE

- Bureaucracy that slows you down

- A revolving door of strategy changes

- A team that doesn't value what SEO actually does



And for the right person, we back it up with an attractive salary package that reflects the true value a great SEO specialist brings to the table.



THE DETAILS

- Employment Type: Full-Time, Permanent

- Hours: Monday to Friday

- Location: Remote

- Remuneration: Attractive salary package on offer



OUR HIRING PROCESS



We take hiring seriously — because we want the best. Shortlisted candidates will be required to complete a skills assessment via TestGorilla to demonstrate their SEO knowledge and problem-solving ability.



Steps:

1. Submit your application to 

2. Initial review of your results and portfolio

3. TestGorilla assessment (shortlisted candidates)

4. Interview with our team

5. Offer to the right person



HOW TO APPLY



Send us your CV, a brief cover letter, and — most importantly — examples of your SEO results. Screenshots, case studies, traffic graphs, ranking movements — show us what you've achieved and how you achieved it. Applications without evidence of results will not be considered.



We're looking for the real deal. If that's you, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Senior VOB Specialist | Remote | $5/hour
description:
We are hiring an experienced Senior VOB Specialist for a long-term independent contractor opportunity.



You must have 5+ years of direct US healthcare experience handling verification of benefits, insurance eligibility, payer communication, and coverage review. Experience working with DME benefit verification is strongly preferred.



This is not an entry-level role and this is not a training position. If you do not have real, hands-on experience with US insurance verification and benefits workflows, please do not apply.



You must be confident with:

• Verification of benefits (VOB) and eligibility checks

• Reviewing active coverage, plan type, and patient responsibility

• Confirming deductibles, out-of-pocket amounts, copays, and coinsurance

• Determining in-network and out-of-network benefits

• Reviewing referral, authorization, and pre-certification requirements

• Verifying DME coverage criteria and payer-specific benefit rules

• Identifying documentation requirements and plan limitations

• Calling US insurance companies and communicating professionally

• Documenting benefits accurately and completely in EMR, PM, and internal systems

• Working in payer portals, EMR, and practice management systems

• Coordinating with clinical, authorization, and billing teams to prevent delays in care or claim denials



This is a remote role working closely with a husband-and-wife team. We are looking for someone professional, dependable, detail-oriented, and comfortable working independently.



Compensation:

• $5/hour



Schedule:

• Monday through Friday

• 9:00 AM to 7:00 PM EST

• 30-minute unpaid break at 1:30 PM EST

• Some weekends required when workload requires it



Requirements:

• 5+ years of direct US healthcare insurance verification, VOB, or revenue cycle experience

• Strong VOB / eligibility and benefits verification experience required

• DME benefits verification experience strongly preferred

• Strong English communication

• Comfortable using RingCentral

• Comfortable working through a remote PC / remote desktop

• Reliable internet and a professional home office

• Available during the required hours



Hiring requirements:

• Smile Checks background screening

• Criminal background verification

• Reference verification

• Contractor onboarding and agreement through Deel



Long-term opportunity:

This is a long-term independent contractor opportunity. We are looking to build an ongoing working relationship with the right person for as long as the arrangement continues to be a good fit for both sides.



Do not apply if:

• You do not have 5+ years of direct US healthcare insurance verification, VOB, or related experience

• You do not have strong hands-on benefits verification experience

• Your background is mainly admin, VA, customer service, or coursework only

• You need training on insurance verification basics



To apply, include:

 1. Total years of US healthcare / VOB experience

 2. Total years handling DME benefit verification

 3. Specialties supported

 4. EMR / PM / payer portal systems used

 5. A short explanation of how you verify benefits and identify patient responsibility

 6. Resume

 7. Voice recording in English

 8. Confirmation that you are comfortable with Smile Checks, Deel, RingCentral, remote PC work, the required schedule, and working with a husband-and-wife team



Required first line of application:

I have 5+ years of direct US healthcare experience with strong verification of benefits experience.

title: Land Wholesaling Operations VA — Real Estate Experience Required
description:
JOB TITLE:

Land Wholesaling Operations VA — Real Estate Experience Required



JOB DESCRIPTION:

We run a land wholesaling business focused on vacant land acquisition and resale across multiple US states. We run high-volume direct mail campaigns to source motivated sellers, acquire properties under contract, and facilitate back-to-back closings with end buyers.

This is a full-time, long-term remote role. We are not looking for a generalist VA. We need someone who understands how real estate wholesaling works — ideally land specifically — and can own the operational side of the business without handholding.

The owner handles all market research, county selection, deal analysis, and final decisions. The VA owns the workflow: mailer coordination, CRM management, seller and buyer communication, title coordination, and buyer marketing.

This role is designed to deepen over time into a specialized land operations position. If you want stable, long-term work with room to grow, this is it.



WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING:

CRM Management



Log all inbound seller responses (phone, web form, mail) same day, M-F

Maintain clean pipeline records from initial contact through closing

Set follow-up tasks and reminders; make sure no leads or milestones fall through

Pull basic pipeline and activity reports as needed



Seller Communication



Handle inbound seller calls during agreed business hours

Follow up with sellers who received offer letters; document all responses

Ask qualifying questions, assess motivation, gather property details

Escalate ready-to-sign leads to the owner



Direct Mail & Outreach



Coordinate with mail vendor to schedule and send seller letters and offer letters each month

Manage neighbor letter campaigns through external vendor; track responses and log in CRM

Check with the property manager at owner's mailing address for mail 1-2x per week; document and route correspondence

Track all mail activity in the CRM



Comps Coordination



Submit comp requests to external service provider for all properties under evaluation

Log results in the deal file and CRM; flag anything unusual for owner review

Serve as the primary contact with external service provider for comps



Title & Closing Coordination (Buy Side)



Once a seller is under contract, contact the title company and initiate escrow

Gather and submit required documentation and entity information via external service provider

Serve as day-to-day contact for title; escalate anything requiring owner decision

Monitor contingency deadlines and title timelines; flag upcoming milestones proactively



Buyer Marketing & Disposition



List properties for sale on selected social media/advertising platforms; manage inquiries and respond to buyers

Build and maintain a buyer list in CRM; follow up with interested parties

Screen buyers; collect proof of funds or financing docs as needed; escalate qualified buyers



Double Close / Back-to-Back Closing Coordination



Open the B-C leg of escrow once a buyer is confirmed

Coordinate closing date, notary setup, and all documentation with title

Coordinate with transactional funders (external) to confirm A-B leg funding; provide HUD/closing statement

Track outstanding checklist items across multiple simultaneous deals

Post-closing: confirm funds received, update CRM, file all deal documents





WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

Required:



Real estate wholesaling experience — you understand the deal cycle from seller contact through closing

Strong CRM discipline — records updated same day, providing a daily report--plan to meet with ownership weekly as well

Clear written and verbal English — you communicate professionally with sellers, title, vendors, and the owner

Self-directed — you flag problems early, don't need micromanagement (after training period completes)

Reliable availability during agreed US business hours

Looking for long-term work, not a short-term placement



Preferred (top of pay range):



Land wholesaling experience specifically — vacant land is different from houses, and candidates with documented land experience are prioritized

Experience with Investment Dominator CRM or a comparable real estate CRM

Experience coordinating double closes or back-to-back closings

Experience working with transactional funders

Experience managing
 Marketplace listings and buyer inquiries





THIS ROLE IS NOT:



A research role. The owner handles market selection and deal analysis.

A general admin position. You need to know what a double close is and how title works in a land deal.

A house wholesaling VA role adapted for land. If your background is exclusively single-family, please note that clearly when you apply.





HOURS & COMPENSATION:



Full-time: 35-40 hours/week, consistent schedule



Candidates with verified land wholesaling experience will be placed at the top of the range





TO APPLY, PLEASE INCLUDE:



Summary of your real estate wholesaling experience — note land experience explicitly if you have it

CRM and tools experience

Closing coordination experience — types of closings and volume

Your proposed hourly rate

One example of a deal you managed from initial contact through closing — your specific role and what happened

2-3 career/business references (letters are ok but it's ideal if these are people we can connect with directly)



Applications without relevant real estate experience will not be considered.

title: E?Commerce Product Page & Content Assistant (Shopify)
description:
I’m looking for a highly organized, detail?oriented Virtual Assistant to help build and maintain product pages across multiple wellness brands in my Shopify store. This role involves applying pre?built product page templates, creating new templates for additional brands, and updating product listings so they are polished, consistent, and ready for publishing.



If you enjoy structured work, have an eye for clean design, and are comfortable working inside Shopify, this is a great long?term opportunity.



Key Responsibilities Include:



Product Page Creation & Updates:

- Apply an existing product page template across all products within a brand.

- Create new product page templates for additional brands using provided examples and guidelines.

- Update product descriptions, features, benefits, and layout to match brand tone and style.

- Format text, sections, and images to ensure consistency across the store.



Draft Product Setup:

- Review draft products in Shopify and prepare them for publishing.

- Add product details, specifications, and lifestyle copy based on brand direction.

- Ensure all product pages meet quality standards before going live.



Image & Content Organization:

- Upload and organize product images.

- Apply simple text overlays using Canva or similar tools (optional but preferred).

- Ensure all images follow brand styling and formatting guidelines.



Brand Consistency:

- Follow established templates and maintain a cohesive look across all product pages.

- Understand and apply different brand voices within the store.

- Keep product information accurate, clean, and conversion?focused.



Requirements

- Experience with Shopify (product setup, editing, and publishing).

- Strong English writing and editing skills.

- Ability to follow templates and brand guidelines with precision.

- Good eye for layout, formatting, and clean design.

- Comfortable working with tools like Canva, Google Drive, and Shopify admin.

- Reliable, organized, and able to manage multiple products at once.

- High attention to detail — accuracy is extremely important.



Preferred (Not Required)

- Experience with e?commerce product copywriting.

- Familiarity with wellness, beauty, or lifestyle brands.

- Basic image editing skills (resizing, cropping, simple text overlays).

- Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.



Work Details:

- Part?time to start, with potential to grow into a long?term ongoing role.

- Flexible hours

- Clear instructions, templates, and examples will be provided for tasks.

title: Part-time Graphic Designer & AI Creative Specialist – Content, Design & Video
description:
Summary

Join the team behind Australia's Cybersecurity Business of the Year 2024 and Cybersecurity Entrepreneur of the Year 2025, and we need a creative person who thinks in AI, is creative, moves fast, and builds brands that get noticed. If that's YOU, we want you to read further.



Company: Cyber Ethos, Australia

Engagement: Remote | Part-Time Contractor / Service Partnership Agreement

Hours: 15 hours per week



About the Role

Cyber Ethos is a leading cybersecurity advisory firm helping boards, CEOs, and executives understand and govern cyber risk. We are looking for a creative powerhouse who thinks in systems, not tasks. This is not a traditional design role. You will be the engine behind our brand visibility across LinkedIn, Meta, X/Twitter, and our website, using AI tools to produce premium content at scale, fast and with impact.



The value you bring is creative judgement and storytelling, not hours spent on repetitive production.



If you are still manually doing everything by hand, this role is not for you.



What You Will Do / Key Responsibilities

1) Design scroll-stopping, on-brand thumbnails for video content, blog posts, and social media campaigns across all platforms

?2) Repurpose long-form video content (webinars, keynotes, interviews) into short-form clips optimised for LinkedIn, Instagram Reels, Meta, and X using AI-assisted editing

?3) Create AI-generated video content using text-to-video tools, bringing thought leadership topics to life visually without traditional production overhead

?4) Build and maintain a content calendar that ensures consistent brand presence across all channels. You will be working very closely with the Digital Marketing and Executive Support Officer.

5) Write and format LinkedIn carousel posts, blog headers, social media graphics, and website visual assets aligned to Dr. Kiran Kewalramani's personal brand and Cyber Ethos positioning

6) Monitor content performance, iterate on creative based on analytics, and proactively suggest new formats and campaign ideas

7) Own the brand visual identity across all touchpoints, ensuring consistency in tone, colour, typography, and message



Role Requirements

1) Minimum 2-3 years of experience in social media content creation or digital design, with a strong portfolio demonstrating AI-assisted creative work

2) Proven track record of growing professional brand presence on LinkedIn specifically

3) Some familiarity with the cybersecurity, technology, or professional services sector is a strong advantage

4) Ability to work autonomously, manage deadlines, and bring ideas without being asked

5) Strong written English for captions, blog intros, and social copy

6) Proactive mindset: you bring ideas to the table, not just execution



AI Toolkit Requirements

This is non-negotiable. You must be proficient (not curious about, but proficient in) using AI toolkit. Some examples (but not limited to) of AI toolkit is provided below. Cyber Ethos may consider getting the subscription for the recommended toolkit



Visual Design & Thumbnails

1) Canva AI / Magic Studio for thumbnails, carousels, social graphics, and brand templates

2) Adobe Firefly + Adobe Express for professional-grade AI image generation and asset creation

?3) Opusclip

4) Midjourney V7 for photorealistic and concept-level brand visuals

?5) DALL-E 3 / ChatGPT image generation for rapid ideation and custom visual prompts



Text-to-Video & Video Production

1) HeyGen for AI avatar-based video content and spokesperson-style videos

?2) Runway ML (Gen-3) for video editing, motion graphics, and scene generation

?3) InVideo AI for converting blog content and scripts into polished short-form videos

?4) Pictory AI for automatic video repurposing from long-form content

?5) Kling AI or Google Veo 3 for cinematic text-to-video generation

6) CapCut AI or Opus clip for short-form video trimming, captioning, and platform-ready formatting

?

Copywriting & Content Intelligence

1) ChatGPT-4o / Claude for caption writing, blog drafts, headline generation, and repurposing briefs

?2) Jasper AI for social media copy and long-form content aligned to brand voice

?3) Perplexity AI for real-time research to keep content accurate and timely



SEO & Performance

1) VidIQ for YouTube and video thumbnail optimisation and SEO tagging

2) Surfer SEO for blog content optimisation and keyword-led structure

?

Scheduling & Distribution

1) Buffer AI or Hootsuite OwlyWriter AI for automated scheduling and cross-platform publishing

?2) LinkedIn Creator tools natively for analytics, newsletter, and post optimisation



Voiceover & Audio

1) ElevenLabs for AI voiceovers on video content without requiring a recording studio

?2) Beatoven AI for AI-generated background music in video content



Requirements

• Proven experience in digital and social media design

• Strong portfolio of marketing or social media assets

• Excellent English communication skills

• Ability to work independently and meet deadlines

• Proficiency in tools such as Adobe Creative Suite, Canva, and Figma/Adobe XD

• Experience with marketing platforms like GoHighLevel is a plus



What Success Looks Like

Within 90 days, the right person will have built a repeatable AI-powered content production system where a single keynote talk, blog post, or podcast episode is automatically transformed into at least 8 to 10 pieces of platform-optimised content, scheduled, published, and tracked, with minimal manual intervention.



How to Apply

1) Submit your application immediately.

2) In your application provide at least 3 examples of AI-generated or AI-assisted content you have created, including at least one short-form video and one social media campaign.

3) Tell us which AI tools you use daily and what your workflow looks like for taking a raw idea from brief to published content.

4) Interviews will be scheduled in March 2026, with the right candidate starting immediately.



Performance Expectations

• Deliver all assigned tasks on time

• Maintain high brand compliance

• Typical output: 7–12 assets per week

• Minimal revisions and proactive creative suggestions

* Attend Weekly meetings

title: Creative Director (Full-Time)
description:
Job Title: Creative Director (Full-Time)

Schedule: Monday to Friday | 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM EST



We are looking for a highly creative and results-driven Creative Director to lead all visual and brand execution across our business. This role is responsible for overseeing and producing high-performing creative assets that drive conversions across paid ads, website, and product presentation.

 when applying. Applications without this will not be considered.

title: Elite Web Designer - Futuristic, Animation-Heavy Landing Pages (GSAP, Three.js, WebGL)
description:
We are building a landing page for an AI-powered operator agent platform, and we need someone who builds websites that make people stop scrolling and say "what the hell was that."



This is NOT a typical landing page job. We are looking for someone who creates web experiences that feel like they belong in a sci-fi movie. Think: wild kinetic animations, 3D elements, particle effects, smooth scroll-driven storytelling, glassmorphism, interactive cursors, fluid transitions, and layouts that nobody has seen before.



WHAT WE NEED:

- A single landing page that looks and feels absolutely futuristic and unlike anything 99% of people have ever encountered on the web

- Heavy use of animation libraries (GSAP, Framer Motion, Lenis, etc.)

- 3D elements or WebGL/Three.js integration where it makes sense

- Scroll-triggered animations and micro-interactions everywhere

- Dark, sleek, high-tech aesthetic with creative typography

- Fully responsive and performant despite the heavy visuals

- Clean code that actually works in production



HOW TO APPLY (read carefully, we filter out copy-paste applications):



1. THE VERY FIRST THING in your response must be links to 2-3 live websites YOU personally built that showcase extreme animation and visual creativity. This is the #1 thing we are evaluating. If your portfolio is standard business websites, this is not the right fit. No links = instant skip.



2. Put your favorite color in the subject line of your application. This tells us you actually read the post and are not blasting generic responses.



3. Brief description of the tools/libraries you used on those portfolio projects.



4. Your approach to balancing wild visuals with performance.



Applications that do not follow these steps will not be reviewed.



IDEAL SKILLS:

- GSAP (GreenSock) advanced timeline animations

- Three.js / WebGL / Spline / Rive

- Framer Motion or other React animation libraries

- Lenis or Locomotive Scroll for smooth scrolling

- CSS animations and custom shaders

- Figma to code

- Next.js or modern React frameworks



This is a paid project with potential for ongoing work if the result blows our minds. We want to see your most creative, boundary-pushing work. Show us what you can do when there are no boring constraints.



Budget: Project-based, negotiable depending on skill level and portfolio quality.

title: Accountant
description:
We are an Australian financial advisory company seeking a highly capable Offshore Accountant to support our accounting operations. We are looking for someone with solid Australian company experience, strong attention to detail, and advanced hands-on experience using Xero platform. 



This role is ideal for an accountant who is confident working independently, understand Australian business and tax processes, and can deliver accurate, timely, and insightful financial support. 



Key Responsibilities: 

- Structure, manage, and track client or business rebates. 

- Process employee payments, including monthly and fortnightly payroll support. 

- Manage cash flow monitoring and reporting. 

- Perform bank reconciliations and general account reconciliation. 

- Support and coordinate audit requirements and documentation. 

- Manage Accounts Payable and Accounts Receivable processes. 

- Assist with budgeting and forecasting activities. 

- Prepare management reports and provide financial insights. 

- Liaise with internal stakeholders and external parties on tax-related matters. 

- Maintain accurate financial records and ensure timely processing of transactions. 

- Identify process improvements to enhance finance efficiency and accuracy. 



Required Experience and Skills: 

- Proven experience working with Australian companies or within an Australian accounting/finance environment. 

- Strong practical experience using Xero, including reconciliations, AP/AR, reporting, and general ledger management. 

- Experience in payroll processing, including monthly and fortnightly payment cycles. 

- Solid understanding of core accounting processes, including reconciliations, cash flow, payables, and receivables. 

- Experience supporting audits and preparing finance-related documentation. 

- Good understanding of Australian business and finance practices. 

- Strong analytical skills and attention to detail. 

- Excellent written and verbal English communication skills. 

- Ability to work remotely, manage deadlines, and maintain confidentiality. 



Preferred Qualifications: 

- Experience in a professional services, financial advisory, accounting, or similar Australian business environment. 

- Exposure to Australian tax or compliance-related processes. 

- Accounting qualification, bookkeeping certification, or equivalent relevant experience. 

- Ability to provide financial insights beyond transactional processing. 



What We Are Looking For: 

- Reliable, proactive, and organised. 

- Comfortable working with an Australian team and Australian business standards. 

- Able to take ownership of finance tasks with minimal supervision. 

- Process-driven, accurate, and committed to meeting deadlines.

title: Shopify Customer Support CSR
description:
The role is for a CSR position, responding to 30+ client enquiries per day via
, Instagram and Meta for an automotive company. 



Here's a longer summary:



We’re a proudly Australian-made automotive brand with a strong reputation for quality and customer experience. We design and manufacture locally, ship worldwide, and continue to grow!



We’re looking for a Customer Experience Lead to be the voice of our brand across
 and social platforms. This is a forward-facing, high-impact role for someone who enjoys connecting with people, helping them get excited about our product, and representing a growing brand with energy and professionalism.



You won’t just be answering tickets, you’ll be helping potential and existing customers when questions arise, guiding them so they can get the most out of our product, and making sure they have experiences that excite them to try it and really enjoy using it. If you love helping people in this way and take pride in representing a growing brand, this role is for you!



Responsibilities:



• Own customer communication across
 and social media, creating a positive and engaging experience



• Manage refunds, disputes, chargebacks, and warranty cases



• Improve and expand our Help Scout knowledge base



• Diagnose and resolve more complex customer issues when they arise



• Identify and track recurring patterns in customer questions, product issues, and opportunities to improve



• Build SOPs for support workflows



• Improve customer education to reduce incoming ticket volume



• Manage Instagram/Facebook DMs and comments



• Post on our socials and interact with the community



• Eventually help hire, train and manage offshore or junior support staff



You Are:



• Friendly, positive, and confident

• Excellent written communicator (native-level English)

• Experienced in ecommerce (Shopify)

• Comfortable being the public voice of a brand

• Curious and enjoys learning the product in depth

• Strong problem-solving skills and able to handle more complex support scenarios or difficult customers

• Excited to grow with a scaling brand and take ownership of customer experience



Nice to Have:

• Help Scout experience

• Automotive Interest / enthusiast



Growth Opportunity



This is a long-term role starting part-time with strong potential to expand as the brand grows. You’ll become a core part of shaping the customer experience and public presence of the brand.







You will be asked to answer the following questions when submitting a proposal:



Why does this role excite you?



Have you managed social media for a brand?



What ecommerce tools/platforms have you worked with?



Describe your recent experience with similar projects



What is your current availability in hours/week?

title: Video Editor / Brand manager

The objective is trust. We are building an audience of San Diego real estate professionals who know, like, and trust us enough to work with us and refer clients.

What You’ll Do

Content Strategy Execution

Distribution

How to Apply

Apply here:

title: STR Operations Assistant
description:
Operations Assistant: Elevate Vacation Homes | U.S.-Based (San Diego, CA)



Read this carefully before applying.



We are a small but growing vacation rental company based in U.S. (San Diego, CA) managing 40+ properties, with 90% located in San Diego, California.

Our team is lean by design:

- 1 CEO

- 1 COO

- 1 CFO

- 2 Operations Assistants



We are now hiring one more Operations Assistant — and this is an important role. We are not hiring in volume. We are hiring one person we can grow with as we scale to 80–100 properties. If you perform well and stay with us, this role will naturally grow into a higher position when we expand.



Important Context (Read This Carefully)

We have been hiring and letting people go over the last 6 months. Not because of pay. Not because of workload. But because we are very selective and have not yet found the right long-term fit.



This role requires:

- Strong communication

- Good judgment

- Ownership

- Reliability under pressure



If you’re confident in your ability, this is a rare opportunity to join early and grow with the company. If you’re unsure, this is not the role for you. This Role Is For One Type of Person Only



You are:

- Fluent in spoken and written English

- Comfortable speaking on the phone with:

- Guests

- Vendors

- Homeowners

- Calm, logical, and clear under pressure

- Able to manage multiple issues at once

- A strong problem solver

- Someone who finishes what they start



You are not:

- A task-only VA

- Someone who avoids calls

- Someone who needs constant direction

- Someone who panics under stress

- Someone who relies on AI to communicate



Communication Expectations (Very Important)

You should expect:

- 5–20 phone calls per day



Daily coordination with:

- Cleaning teams

- Maintenance vendors

- Internal staff



If you are not confident speaking English on calls, do not apply.



What You’ll Actually Be Doing: This is not a customer service role.

Approximate breakdown:

- 10% Guest communication

- 5% Cleaning reminders

- 5% Ordering supplies

- 80% Operations, coordination, and problem-solving



You will function as:

- A project manager

- A communicator between teams

- A problem-solver when things go wrong

- A key operations support role



Work Environment

- Small, fast-moving startup

- High accountability

- Clear expectations

- Real responsibility

- Direct communication with leadership

- Opportunity to grow as the company scales



Compensation & Benefits

- Up to $8.00 USD/hour (based on performance)

- 10 days paid time off per year

- 2 days off per week

- Long-term role with growth potential

- Promotion opportunity as we scale to 80–100 properties

title: Loan Processor - Real Estate
description:
About the Role

We are seeking a detail-oriented and experienced Loan Processor to join our growing financial services team. In this role, you will be the backbone of our lending operations — reviewing, verifying, and processing loan applications from submission through closing. You will collaborate closely with loan officers, underwriters, and clients to ensure accurate, timely, and compliant loan packages.



Key Responsibilities:

01 Review and verify loan applications for completeness, accuracy, and compliance with lending policies and regulatory requirements.

02 Collect and organize required documentation including income verification, credit reports, tax returns, employment records, and appraisals.

03 Order and track third-party services such as appraisals, title searches, flood certifications, and credit supplements.

04 Communicate with borrowers, real estate agents, and loan officers to obtain outstanding conditions and ensure timely loan closings.

05 Identify and resolve discrepancies or red flags in loan documentation, escalating issues to underwriters or supervisors as needed.

06 Coordinate loan closing activities including preparation of closing disclosures, settlement statements, and final loan packages.



Minimum Qualifications:

- Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field

- Minimum 2 years of loan processing, banking, or credit-related experience

- Proficiency in Loan Origination Systems (LOS) and MS Office Suite

- Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills

- Effective written and verbal communication in Filipino and English

- Ability to manage multiple loan files and meet tight deadlines



Benefits:

* Fixed Salary

* Unlimited Incentives
